title: Re-enter the wells others have written off.
intro: Damaged casing, stuck packers, fish and lost surface access don't have to mean a lost well. Gunnar combines magnetic ranging, outside-in milling and directional drilling to re-establish access to complex wellbores — for production, integrity or abandonment.

heroStats.l: ranging accuracy to contact the target
CTRWD™ tool for tight, deep tubulars
fish and obstructions via outside-in re-entry

challengeTitle: When the wellbore won't let you back in.
challenge: Obstructions, collapsed casing, stuck tubing and unrecorded sidetracks can make a wellbore impossible to re-enter by conventional means — stranding production, integrity work or abandonment.
High-inclination and motherbore exit/re-entry scenarios push standard rig and wireline operations past their limits.
approachTitle: Range to the target, then mill back in.
approach: Gunnar uses active ranging to locate the target wellbore precisely, then mills in from outside to re-establish communication — bypassing fish and obstructions that block a conventional path.
Compact, under-pressure coiled-tubing systems handle high-inclination and deep, small-tubular re-entries where standard operations struggle, with continuous well-control barriers in place.

capabilities.b: Active and passive ranging to find the wellbore to re-enter.
Mill into the target casing to bypass fish and obstructions.
High-inclination exit and re-entry beyond standard rig limits.
Re-establish a path past stuck tools and lost equipment.
Coiled-tubing deployment with continuous well-control barriers.
Restore the well for production, integrity work or permanent P&A.

tech.name: CTRWD™
tech.note: 3.5" coiled-tubing ranging-while-drilling — mills, exits, ranges, steers and re-enters under pressure.

tech.name: DeadSet™
tech.note: No-access active ranging with ±1 ft accuracy to contact and re-enter the target.

tech.name: ClearCut™
tech.note: Milling simulation that turns intercept geometry into real-world milling parameters.

caseTitle: Outside-in re-entry of a complex wellbore
caseBody: Where standard rig operations struggle, Gunnar's compact CTRWD™ system mills, exits, ranges, steers and re-enters to reach smaller tubulars found in deep, complex wells — eliminating wireline runs and re-establishing access for permanent sealing.
caseMetricL: exit and re-entry beyond standard limits

faqs.q: What makes a well hard to re-enter?
faqs.a: Collapsed casing, stuck packers, fish (lost tools), unrecorded sidetracks and high inclination can all block conventional re-entry. Ranging-guided outside-in milling provides a path back in when standard methods fail.
Gunnar drills a new path, uses active ranging to contact the target casing precisely, then mills in from outside to re-establish communication — bypassing whatever is blocking the original bore.

faqs.q: Can you work in high-inclination wells?
faqs.a: Yes. Compact coiled-tubing systems like CTRWD™ are designed for high-inclination and motherbore exit/re-entry where standard rig and wireline operations struggle.
Coiled-tubing deployment allows operations 'under pressure' with continuously present well-control barriers that aren't possible during conventional rig or wireline work.
